---
title: "ePortfolio: Reflection"
author: "Daniel Vala"
date: "2026-04-22"
description: "A reflection on the skills and knowledge acquired throughout the BAA1028 Workflow & Data Management module."
image: ../../assets/images/reflection-thumb.jpg
categories: [Reflection, Web Development, Git]
---

<div style="height: 1.5rem;"></div>

This module introduced me to several tools and concepts that were entirely new to me. I learned the basics of Git and GitHub for version control, which I can see being useful in any role related to data or coding in the future. Furthermore, the module gave me a solid introduction to web development and web design - Quarto, HTML, CSS, and Sass - which allowed me to understand how websites are built and styled.

Perhaps the most valuable takeaway was realising how easy and accessible it is to create and publish your own website. Using Quarto, GitHub Pages, and a little bit of Sass/SCSS code for styling, I was able to build a professional-looking site without any prior web development experience and at no cost. This is something I find genuinely inspiring and plan to make use of in the future.

This project also gave me the opportunity to practice working with AI in a coding context. I used AI assistance throughout the development process, which significantly sped up my learning and allowed me to go even beyond what was taught in lectures. I believe this is particularly relevant, as collaborating with AI is very likely to become a standard part of software development in the near future. Learning how to communicate with AI tools effectively, verify their output, and integrate their suggestions into my own workflow is a skill I want to continue building on.

I would also like to mention the Kubicle courses on presentation, which I found very interesting. I will definitely think of including concepts like the Pyramid Principle or the MECE framework next time I do a presentation. The storytelling concepts were also inspiring, and I think they have changed my view of presentations overall - something I definitely did not expect before watching the courses.

Overall, this module was very different from all the other modules at college. I really liked how it combined theoretical concepts, such as how Git or different coding languages like HTML or CSS work, with immediately putting them into practice. And furthermore, the idea that at the end of the module I will have my personal website that I can use in the future is really great.
